pr_title: fix(compile): skip Node-to-Deno arg translation in standalone binaries
prompt: When a `deno compile`d standalone binary spawns itself using `child_process.spawn(process.execPath, args)`, Deno's child_process polyfill detects that the file matches `Deno.execPath()` and translates Node.js-style arguments (like `--max-old-space-size=4096`) into Deno CLI arguments (`run -A --unstable-node-globals --unstable-bare-node-builtins --unstable-detect-cjs --v8-flags=...`). In standalone binaries, these injected Deno flags end up in `process.argv`, causing argument parsers like yargs to fail with 'Unknown arguments' errors. Fix this by checking `Deno.build.standalone` before applying the Node-to-Deno arg translation in two places: (1) the `buildCommand()` function in `ext/node/polyfills/internal/child_process.ts` for `spawn`, and (2) the `fork()` function in `ext/node/polyfills/child_process.ts`. Standalone binaries already have permissions, unstable config, and V8 flags baked in, so translation is unnecessary. Add a spec test that compiles a binary which spawns itself and verifies no Deno flags leak into the child's `process.argv`.
